data.title: SBM Offshore to Exit Equatorial Guinea with Aseng Vessel Sale
data.description:
SBM Offshore said Wednesday it agreed to sell its equity interest in the lease and operating entities of the Aseng floating production storage and offloading vessel to GEPetrol, the national oil company of Equatorial Guinea.

The exit from Equatorial Guinea will occur after a transition period of up to 12 months. The move aligns with the offshore energy company's ongoing strategy to streamline its Lease and Operate portfolio.

The deal is subject to standard conditions and regulatory approvals.

data.title: Silver Holds Near 7-Month Highs on Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
Silver prices hovered around $34.50 per ounce on Thursday, near their highest levels in seven months, as a wave of disappointing US economic data weighed on the dollar and boosted demand for safe-haven assets.

The ADP report released Wednesday showed the US private sector added just 37,000 jobs in May—well below expectations and the weakest reading in over two years.

Additionally, the ISM services PMI signaled contraction for the first time in nearly a year, with a sharp drop in new business and rising input costs, likely linked to new tariffs.

President Donald Trump continued to pressure the Federal Reserve to cut rates, but officials have so far favored a cautious approach amid heightened uncertainty.

On the trade front, Trump further dampened sentiment by saying it was “extremely hard” to reach a deal with Chinese President Xi Jinping, despite earlier indications that the two leaders may speak this week.

data.title: Market Chatter: Airbus' Chief Procurement Officer to Lead India Operations
data.description:
Airbus' Chief Procurement Officer Juergen Westermeier was reportedly appointed to lead the company's operations in India and South Asia, effective Sept. 1.

The French aerospace company did not name his successor, saying the matter is "subject to further notice," Reuters wrote Wednesday, citing an internal memo.

Westermeier succeeds Remi Maillard, who was appointed as the company's head of technology, according to the news outlet.

A source close to the company told the publication that Westermeier's move is not connected to recent supply chain issues.

Airbus did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Newgen Software Technologies Bags New Order Worth $2.5 Million
data.description:
Newgen Software Technologies has secured an order worth $2.5 million from an undisclosed international client for the procurement of an enterprise workflow and content management system, according to a Wednesday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The order includes the supply of Newgen's product and its implementation for the customer's digital transformation across multiple lines of business.

The project will be executed in five years, the filing said.

data.title: Fitch Maintains BNP Paribas' Ratings on 'Stable' Business Profile
data.description:
Fitch Ratings on Wednesday affirmed its long-term issuer default rating on BNP Paribas at A+, with a stable outlook.

The rating action is backed by the French lender's "stable and diversified" business profile, as well as its strong presence in the European commercial and retail banking sector.

It also reflects the bank's solid asset quality and its relatively large revenue base, making it more resilient to economic shocks, according to the rating agency.

data.title: China's yuan holds firm as traders await US-China talks
data.description:
China's yuan hovered around its strongest level against the U.S. dollar in nearly two weeks on Thursday, buoyed by persistent weakness in the greenback as markets awaited further clarity on trade negotiations and potential tariff developments.

The yuan eased 0.1% after touching 7.1720, its strongest since May 26. Its offshore counterpart was also down about 0.1% in Asian trade.

Investors were cautious after U.S. President Donald Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping was tough and "extremely hard to make a deal with". Markets are now awaiting the results of a phone call between the two leaders this week and any further insights into potential tariff developments.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China set the midpoint rate at 7.1865 per dollar, 103 pips weaker than a Reuters estimate, signalling the central bank's intent to maintain stability in the yuan and temper rapid appreciation.

The spot yuan is allowed to trade 2% either side of the fixed midpoint each day. Based on Thursday's official guidance, the yuan is allowed to drop as far as 7.3302.

The currency is up 0.3% against the dollar this month and 1.6% firmer this year as broad weakness in the U.S. dollar adds to the strengthening trend.

"Looking beyond near-term tariff headlines, we think the CNY may now be commencing a multi-year strengthening path against the USD," analysts at Goldman Sachs wrote in a note.

Elsewhere, the Hong Kong dollar remained near the weak end of a band around 7.85 to the dollar. The overnight interbank offered rate (HIHKDOND=) rebounded to 0.01949% from a record low in the biggest single-day increase since 2023, while interbank rates for one-month (HIHKD1MD=) and three-month (HIHKD3MD=) continued to hover near three-year lows.

The dollar's six-currency index was stuck near a six-week low, after data showed that the U.S. services sector had contracted for the first time in nearly a year, reigniting concerns about sluggish growth and persistent inflation.

Key onshore vs offshore levels:

* Overnight dollar/yuan swap onshore -5.70 pips vs. offshore -5.70

* Three-month SHIBOR (SHIBOR=) 1.7% vs. 3-month CNH HIBOR 1.8%

data.title: U.K. Property Sales at Four-Year High in May
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Property sales in the U.K. saw their busiest May since 2021, highlighting the improvement in market conditions, according to Rightmove.

The property website said the number of sales across the U.K. was 6% ahead of the same period in 2024, albeit with marked differences between regions. May was the busiest of any month since March 2022.

In Wales, the number of sales agreed was 15% higher, compared with London, where the figure was just 1% higher.

Heywood, in the greater Manchester area, saw a near doubling in the number of buyers agreeing to purchase a home from the same time last year at an 88% increase. Pudsey, in West Yorkshire, was second on the list at 85% growth, with sales in Cheshire's Wilmslow up 66%, According to Rightmove.

Still, nine out of the top 10 sales hotspots outside of London in May had an average price tag which was below the national average, an indication that affordability has influenced sales trends. The average five-year fixed mortgage rate is now 4.61%, compared with 6.11% at the peak in July 2023, Rightmove says.

The highest average price for a home outside of London was found in Wilmslow, at 569,694 pounds ($770,055), followed by Lichfiled at 372,494 pounds, and Brislington at 359,475 pounds.

The highest average price was seen in London, with Hampstead leading the ranking at 1,367,757 pounds, followed by Surrey's Kingston Upon Thames at 781,977 pounds, and north London's Barnet, at 735,218 pounds.

According to Rightmove, the national average asking price for May was 379,517 pounds.

"With mortgage rates higher than the lows we saw post financial crisis, and affordability stretched, many buyers are continuing to look for value, which is likely reflected in the lower-than-average prices of some of the current hotspots," Rightmove said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Switch 2 debuts but no fun-and-games in trade
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rocky Swift

It's Switch 2 Day! The much-anticipated sequel to Nintendo's immensely successful portable gaming unit goes on sale around the world on Thursday. But don't bother trying to find one: They're all sold out.

The Switch 2 is manufactured mostly in China and sold out of Japan, so it's anybody's guess when there will be more of them available and what they'll cost, given all the uncertainty over tariffs and supply chains.

A United States deadline for "best offers" on trade came and went on Wednesday without any trade announcements, and President Donald Trump continued to stir up controversy on the global stage with a proclamation banning nationals of 12 countries from the U.S.

But the trade talks go on, with Japan sending its head trade negotiator Ryosei Akazawa to the U.S. again today in search of a deal. Germany's new chancellor, Friedrich Merz, is also headed to Washington for some face time with Trump in the Oval Office.

The main event today will be the European Central Bank's interest rate decision, which is almost certain to cut rates by 25 basis points. The post-decision comments by President Christine Lagarde will be all the more important for clues on future policy moves.

Stock futures pointed to flat openings for both European and U.S. markets.

Key developments that could influence markets on Thursday:

- ECB decision, speech by Christine Lagarde

- German Chancellor Friedrich Merz travels to Washington

- German data on industrial orders, consumer goods for April

- U.S. data on jobless claims for end of May, trade data for April

- Fed's Jeffrey Schmid, Patrick Harker, Adriana Kugler to speak

Trying to keep up with the latest tariff news?

Our new daily news digest offers a rundown of the top market-moving headlines impacting global trade. Sign up for Tariff Watch here.

data.title: SGX Sicom RSS3 Rubber Untraded at Midday
SGX Sicom Benchmark July TSR20 Rubber Down 1.3 Cents at 159.7 U.S. Cents/Kg
Finland's Valmet Maintains FY25 Outlook
data.description:
Valmet affirmed its financial guidance for full-year 2025, expecting net sales to be at the same level as the prior year.

The pulp and paper industrial technology group posted net sales of 5.36 billion euros in 2024, according to a Wednesday release.

Looking further ahead, Valmet expects organic net sales growth of 5% by 2030, compared with a prior forecast of over two times the market growth.

data.title: NZME Price Target Raised 0.8% to NZ$1.26/Share by Forsyth Barr
Pacific Current Group to Monitor Investment in Abacus Global Management, Shares Fall 5%
data.description:
Pacific Current Group said it will continue to monitor its investment in Abacus Global Management and that it was aware of a media article published by Morpheus Research regarding the firm on Wednesday, as well as the firm's response, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Pacific Current currently holds securities in Abacus Life.

Abacus Global said it consistently produced positive realized returns in its response to a "false and misleading short attack."

Pacific Current Group's shares fell 5% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hong Kong's Towngas Sees No Plan to Increase Tariffs, Managing Director Says
data.description:
Hong Kong and China Gas Managing Director Peter Wong Wai-yee said there are no plans to adjust its prices despite trade uncertainties, The Standard reported Wednesday, citing remarks during Towngas' annual general meeting.

For Wong, the best course of action is to keep the dividend at HK$0.35, the report said.

The executive forecast gas sales in the city to slip by less than 1% year over year in 2025 due to an increase in temperatures and the trend of Hongkongers going north to the mainland, The Standard reported.

Gas usage in households increased by 160% gigajoules due to a fall in temperatures, while usage in hotels and airport catering increased due to a rebound in events and aviation activity, The Standard said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

** Keytruda, which belongs to a class of immunotherapy called PD-L1 inhibitor, had global sales of $29.5 bln in 2024 - REDY

data.title: Japan's Wage Growth Likely to Slow Due to Tariff Threat — Market Talk
data.description: Japan's pay growth will likely slow in the coming months as higher tariffs hit production and shipments, says Moody's Analytics economist Stefan Angrick. Inflation-adjusted wages dropped 1.8% from a year earlier in April, according to government data released Thursday. Continued inflation "spells trouble for household consumption and raises the stakes ahead of July's Upper House election," Angrick says in a research note. "With wage growth stumbling and inflation proving sticky, things will only get more difficult from here," he adds. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Philippines' Headline Consumer Inflation Further Slows to 1.3% in May
data.description:
The Philippines' headline inflation eased to 1.3% in May from 1.3% in the previous month, according to data from the Philippine Statistics Authority on Thursday.

The lower inflation was mainly driven by a slower annual increment in the index of housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels at 2.3% in May from 2.9% in the previous month. Another contributor was a faster annual drop in the restaurant and accommodation services at 2% from 2.3% in the month prior.

Food inflation remained unchanged 0.7% in May versus April, the PSA said.

Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, remained unchanged at 2.2% in May versus the month prior, the data showed.

data.title: Worldline to Repurchase EUR320 Million of Convertible Bonds
data.description:
Worldline SA agreed to repurchase 320 million euros worth of its convertible bonds due July 30, 2026, after completing a reverse bookbuilding process on Wednesday, according to a same-day release.

The French payments company will buy back 3,221,238 bonds at 99.40 euros each, representing around 41.6% of the originally issued bonds with a principal amount of 332.4 million euros.

Settlement is expected on June 11, with the repurchased bonds to be cancelled.

data.title: Tech shares drive Hong Kong and China stocks higher
data.description:
Hong Kong and China stocks rose on Thursday, led by tech and artificial intelligence shares, as analysts said Hong Kong-listed tech firms remain under-represented in global AI investment portfolios.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 Index and the Shanghai Composite Index both edged up 0.1% by the lunch break. Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng was up 0.4%.

** Hong Kong's equity market offers strategic value for global investors, serving as a key option for diversifying portfolios and navigating the ongoing shift away from the U.S. dollar, Huatai analysts said in a note.

** The analysts said technology remains a central investment theme. "As future gains in global productivity are expected to hinge on advances in artificial intelligence, the companies best positioned to lead this race are largely concentrated in the U.S. and Hong Kong," they said, adding that Hong Kong remains under-allocated.

** Tech shares traded in Hong Kong were up 0.9%, tracking gains in Chinese ADRs listed in New York overnight, while onshore tech shares (.CSIASITI) climbed 1.0%.

** China's services activity expanded at a slightly faster pace in May, with new orders growing more quickly than in April, though new export orders declined due to uncertainty stemming from U.S. tariffs, a private sector survey showed on Thursday.

** The CSI Rare Earth Index rose 0.7%, after a group representing auto suppliers in the United States called for immediate action to address China's restricted exports of rare earths, minerals and magnets, warning the issue could quickly disrupt auto parts production.

** YTD, stock up 36.8%

data.title: Philippines May Inflation Data Backs Case for Another Rate Cut — Market Talk
data.description: Philippines' May inflation print reinforces UOB's view that the BSP will cut the overnight reverse repurchase rate by another 25 bps later this month, UOB economist Loke Siew Ting writes in a note. The easing path will then depend on global tariff and financial market developments, as well as upcoming inflation and domestic growth data, she says. In May, headline inflation undershot the central bank's target range for the third straight month. UOB maintains its 2025 inflation forecast for the Philippines at 2.0%, given the non-monetary intervention by the national government. There are also expectations of lower global oil prices and a stronger peso for the rest of this year, the economist says.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Fresh Linear low density polyethylene (LLDPE Powder) offers in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa reported that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its LLDPE Powder (MG500026) grade at the USD 1285/mt levels.This offer is for June 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: China Carbon Neutral Development Appoints Ex-UN Adviser as Co-Chairman
data.description:
China Carbon Neutral Development appointed Chen Yonglan as co-chairman and non-executive director of the board, effective June 4, according to a Wednesday Hong Kong stock exchange filing.

Chen is a senior adviser to the United Nations and brings over 30 years of global experience in diplomacy, investment, and ESG. He previously held roles at the UNDP, Invest Sweden, French PE firm Eurazeo, and, most recently, Fullerton Fund Management, the filing said.

data.title: Copper Holds Advance on Supply Concerns
data.description:
Copper futures held near $4.90 per pound on Thursday, remaining up nearly 5% so far this week, as persistent supply-side disruptions and rising trade tensions fueled bullish sentiment.

Production setbacks at Teck Resources' operations in Chile are expected to last for several months, while mining activity at the Kamoa-Kakula project in the Democratic Republic of Congo has also been disrupted—tightening global supply.

Meanwhile, investor anxiety grew after President Trump signed an executive order doubling tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50%, sparking speculation that copper could be next.

The US is currently conducting an investigation into copper imports, adding to uncertainty.

However, upside momentum was tempered by weaker-than-expected manufacturing and services data from China, the world’s top copper consumer, raising concerns about demand softness.

data.title: Australia's PainChek drops most in nearly 8 years on FDA approval delay
data.description:
** PainChek plunges as much as ~31% to A$0.038; marks biggest intraday pct drop since June 18, 2017

** Stock hits lowest since May 7

** Medical devices maker flags potential delay in U.S. FDA clearance for its pain assessment app

** Expects clearance by mid-to-late September instead of the first half of 2025 anticipated earlier

** About 14.3 mln shares traded in stock's busiest day since February 18, 2021

** PCK last up 21.8%, taking YTD gains to 48.3%

data.title: Market Chatter: Banco Santander Names Chief Accounting Officer Replacement Amid Litigation Concerns
data.description:
Banco Santander reportedly replaced Alexsandro Broedel Lopes with Manuel Preto to take on the role of its new chief accounting officer, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing an internal memo it saw.

The move comes after Broedel, who was set to assume the Spanish lender's accounting chief role in June, was accused of breaching policies and funds diversion by Brazilian bank Itau Unibanco.

Preto's appointment is set to take effect on July 31, the report noted.

Banco Santander did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.
